LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT M-JSF INVESTMENTS LLC DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING THEY WERE TENANTS IN JANUARY 2006 WHEN DEFENDANT INVOKED THE ELLIS ACT. NO FACTS PLED SHOWING THAT THE CLAIMS ARE NOT TIME BARRED. DELAYED DISCOVERY RULE NOT APPLICABLE BECAUSE PLAINTIFFS HAD CONSTRUCTIVE NOTICE OF ANY ALLEGED VIOLATION OF THE RENT ORDINANCES WHEN DEFENDANT FILED ITS NOTICE OF INTENT INVOKING THE ELLIS ACT ON JANUARY 31, 2006. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: SCOTT HUGHES, CSR #12365

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Matters Admitted; Sanction Requested
Off Calendar Jul-29-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, Defendant M-JSF Investments, LLC's motion to deem matters admitted, is taken off calendar, at the request of the moving party. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-17-2009 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-01-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT M-JSF INVESTMENTS LLC DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Karen Uchiyama, Esq. Law Offices of Karen Y. Uchiyama, Law Offices of Karen Uchiyama (415) 563-9300, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; James Brandan Kraus, Esq. of Zacks & Utrecht (415) 956-8100, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiffs fail to allege facts showing they were tenants in January 2006 when defendant invoked the Ellis Act. No facts pled showing that the claims are not time barred. Delayed Discovery Rule not applicable because plaintiffs had constructive notice of any alleged violation of the Rent Ordinances when defendant filed its notice of intent invoking the Ellis Act on January 31, 2006. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Scott Hughes, CSR # 12365

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-01-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT M-JSF INVESTMENTS LLC MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Moot in view of ruling on Demurrer. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Scott Hughes, CSR # 12365

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-28-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT M-J SF INVESTMENTS LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: James B. Kraus, of Zacks & Utrecht (415) 956-8100, appearing for Defendant and moving party; Karen Y. Uchiyama, Law Offices of Karen Y. Uchiyama (415) 563-9300, appearing for Plaintiff. Both Counsel stipulate to the Court's tentative, and, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFFS TO ALLEGE FACTS THAT SHOW THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE NOT TIME-BARRED, AND THAT THEY WERE "TENANTS" IN JANUARY 2006 WHEN DEFENDANT INVOKED THE ELLIS ACT. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW/VC)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

MOTION TO STRIKE
Off Calendar Apr-28-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT M-J SF INVESTMENTS LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT. Appearances: James B. Kraus, of Zacks & Utrecht (415) 956-8100, appearing for Defendant and moving party; Karen Y. Uchiyama, Law Offices of Karen Y. Uchiyama (415) 563-9300, appearing for Plaintiff. Both Counsel stipulate to the Court's tentative, and, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW/VC)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
